Learn React Now — Wrap-up Notes & Full Table of Contents (30)

This is the last post in my "You'll Have to Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?" series, and it also works as the table of contents. So I figured I'd close things out by sharing how the learning went and a few thoughts I picked up along the way.

Honestly, I usually work with Vue. I'd been telling myself for a while that I should sit down and actually learn React when I had the time, but that time never showed up. So I used this challenge as the excuse to finally do it. The whole series was written straight from reading the official docs, which means some parts might not be perfectly accurate, or might even be flat-out wrong. If you spot anything, feel free to leave a comment below and let me know.
Looking back at this challenge, I feel like the first 18 or so posts were a bit more structured. After that, I'll be honest, the writing got kind of thin toward the end lol.
Anyway, enough rambling. The table of contents is below, so if any of it catches your eye, just click through and take a look.
